steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the $y$-axis at $y=1.5$ (a positive $y$-intercept) and slopes downward from left to right, so its slope is negative.

steps.title: Convert each choice to slope-intercept form
steps.content: Solve each equation for $y$ and use $R>0$ to determine the sign of the slope and the value of the $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $Rx+12y=18 \Rightarrow y=-\frac{R}{12}x+\frac{3}{2}$. Slope $-\frac{R}{12}<0$ and $y$-intercept $1.5$. Matches the graph.
  • B) $Rx-12y=-18 \Rightarrow y=\frac{R}{12}x+\frac{3}{2}$. Slope is positive. Eliminated.
  • C) $Rx+12y=-18 \Rightarrow y=-\frac{R}{12}x-\frac{3}{2}$. $y$-intercept is $-1.5$. Eliminated.
  • D) $Rx-12y=18 \Rightarrow y=\frac{R}{12}x-\frac{3}{2}$. Slope positive and $y$-intercept negative. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
